mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing equity investors to price in lower earnings and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or quarterly forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization gains and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in >60% of acceleration episodes, or the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 190.0, inflationary pressures erode real corporate margins and force anticipated monetary tightening, resulting in negative equity forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of threshold-crossing months, or the predictive power decays to Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative in >50% of co-occurring months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product directly increases aggregate corporate profit pools, establishing a positive fundamental valuation floor that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following GDP growth, or the correlation between GDP growth and equity returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, prompting equity deleveraging and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of contraction episodes, or the effect is fully subsumed by monetary policy variables in joint estimation.
